title: Data Science Capstone
subtitle: DATA 399
description: The Capstone course is designed to provide students in the College of Emerging and Collaborative Studies with an opportunity to apply knowledge, skills, and methodologies from their studies to a significant, independent project, while working in interdisciplinary teams of 3-4 students.

instructor.name: Dr. Amir Sadovnik
instructor.bio: Dr. Sadovnik is the Research Lead for the newly established Center for AI Security Research (CAISER) at Oak Ridge National Lab. As part of this role, Dr. Sadovnik leads multiple research projects related to AI risk, adversarial AI, and large language model vulnerabilities.

learningOutcomes: Conduct independent research or a creative project in their field of study.
Integrate and apply theoretical knowledge to real-world issues or professional scenarios.
Develop a well-structured final project that demonstrates subject expertise.
Communicate project findings eﬀectively, both in written and oral formats.
Collaborate with peers and receive feedback constructively.

policies.submission: All work must be submitted via Canvas. These will usually be by 11:59 PM EST on the due date. Late submissions will not be accepted. While some assignments will be independent others will be done in groups
policies.honesty: Students are allowed to use LLMs (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) as coding assistants, but must disclose their usage. Also, students must make sure they understand all code they have written. Plagiarism of academic papers or other students work will result in immediate failure.
policies.attendance: This is a seminar-style course. Active participation is required. Attendance is expected. Please read all assigned readings *before* class.
policies.additional: Please make sure to find out the last day to drop/withdraw from the course if needed. For campus-wide policies on disability accommodations and mental health resources, please visit https://teaching.utk.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/78/2026/01/Campus-Syllabus-Updated-1-8-26.2.docx

assignments.title: Status Updates
assignments.desc: Every week you will be required to present a few slides of the progress made that week. A template for that will be provided.

assignments.title: Project Posts
assignments.desc: Every three weeks you will be requires to write a longer update of the challenges you faced and how you solved them. Format will be discussed in class.

assignments.title: Senior Project
assignments.desc: Most of the class will revolve around your final senior project. The final submission will include an oral presentation, a website, and a report.

prereqs: (let me know if you have not done those)

weeks.desc: Selecting capstone idea and getting faculty approval.
Students will develop a clear requirements specification and description of the project.
a) work plan; b) work division c) datasets d) programming language/software e) how to test application.
Continuing work on the Capstone according to the above plan. Students must meet weekly with the professor to discuss interim results and get feedback and suggestions from the professor.
Complete testing, submittal of the final write-up, including finished product. Every group presents their project to the rest of the class.
